provenance: Witzel 2000 'Home of the Aryans' places in eastern Iran/upper Oxus region; Gnoli 1980 'Zoroaster's Time and Homeland' in Pamir foothills. We use a compromise mid-point. Sensitivity analysis tests alternative placements.
Gnoli 1980; Witzel 2000. Modern Samarkand, Uzbekistan. Universally identified.
Gnoli 1980. Ancient Merv, modern Mary, Turkmenistan. Universally identified.
Gnoli 1980; Witzel 2000. Ancient Balkh / Bactra, northern Afghanistan. Universally identified.
Gnoli 1980 places between Margiana and Bactria; specific town disputed. Some place near Maymana, others further east. Mid-point of Merv-Balkh corridor used.
Gnoli 1980; Witzel 2000. Ancient Areia, modern Herat region, western Afghanistan.
Gnoli 1980 identifies as Kabul region. Some scholars (Witzel) note potential association with Gandhāra further east. Kabul-centroid used.
Gnoli 1980 tentatively places in eastern Iran; identification disputed. Sensitivity analysis tests Mashhad alternative (~59°E, 36°N).
Gnoli 1980 identifies with Vərkana / Hyrcania, SE Caspian. Modern Gorgan plain, northern Iran.
Gnoli 1980; Witzel 2000. Universally identified with Arachosia, modern Kandahar region.
Gnoli 1980. The Helmand river basin, Sistan region. Universally identified.
Gnoli 1980. Ancient Rhagae, modern Rey south of Tehran.
Gnoli 1980 places tentatively in Khorasan. Alternative readings exist. Sensitivity analysis tests alternatives.
Witzel 2000 tentatively associates with Buner / Hazara region of NW Pakistan; mythologically the home of Θraētaona. Other readings place further west.
Universally identified with the Punjab Sapta-Sindhu of the Rigveda. Centroid of Punjab plain.

Most uncertain. Gnoli 1980 surveys options: Volga (~45°E, 50°N), Jaxartes/Syr Darya (~65°E, 42°N), or Indus (~68°E, 28°N). Mythic/cosmic river; we use Jaxartes as default mid-option. Sensitivity analysis tests all three.
